Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is the chance to build a recruiting function—not just run one. I love the full arc: understanding what a candidate actually wants, communicating in a way that out-competes bigger brands, and using data to keep getting sharper. At Kronos I worked a tough international role full-cycle—drafting the JD, posting, sourcing, and running personalized outreach that increased applications by 566% in two months—and I learned how much closing depends on genuine understanding and warmth. Being Mintlify's second Talent hire, working directly with founders, is exactly the high-ownership, build-it-from-scratch seat I want.
Tell us about your recruiting / sourcing: I'll be candid—my deepest recruiting experience is at Kronos Research, where I owned a hard-to-fill international role end-to-end: writing the JD, posting and sourcing, researching global comp, and running compelling outreach that drove a 566% jump in applications. I also organized recruiting events and moderated C-suite panels, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants. Since then, my work at InGenius and in OD consulting has sharpened the adjacent muscles a great recruiter needs—stakeholder management, metrics-driven iteration, and warm communication. I haven't owned a full-cycle req load at a startup yet, but I'm genuinely energized by sourcing and closing, and I learn fast.
How do you use data / metrics in recruit: My greatest strength is relying on data to solve problems and build sustainable strategy around what stakeholders actually need. At InGenius I drive metrics-informed decisions—using enrollment and feedback data to experiment, iterate, and improve outcomes—and at NextGen I analyzed 2,000+ employee responses to surface what really moves engagement. In recruiting, I'd apply the same instinct: track funnel metrics, A/B test outreach copy for response rates, and present findings to hiring managers to shorten time-to-hire without sacrificing candidate experience.
How do you think about using AI in recru: I'm genuinely into this—I use AI tools daily and have been building a RAG-based system on the side that evaluates job fit from my own resumes and experience. In recruiting, I see AI's best use as removing tedious work (drafting templated comms, screening logistics, surfacing data insights) so I can spend more time on the high-judgment, human parts: personalizing outreach and closing. The goal is to make the process faster without ever reducing the quality of a candidate's experience.
Why Mintlify?: I'm drawn to Mintlify because Talent isn't a talking point here—the team voted it the single most important thing to protect as you scale, and as the second Talent hire I'd be directly responsible for upholding that. I also love that you'd start me as a generalist with a real seat at the table. My graduate training in Applied Organizational Psychology means I treat hiring as a system to study and improve, and I'd be excited to build that foundation with founders who clearly care about getting it right.
